sentence: Subjective:

HPI: The patient, a 60-year-old male, has been experiencing chronic back pain for approximately 2 years, initially occurring after moving furniture, with a sharp stabbing pain in the back and radiating to the right leg, including the big toe.
The pain worsens with sitting and is accompanied by numbness and tingling in the right leg.
The patient has tried physical therapy and strong pain medications in the past, but with temporary relief.
He has also been advised about surgery but prefers to avoid it.
Past medical history: The patient quit smoking 20 years ago and does not recall any family members with spine conditions.
Review of systems: The patient experiences difficulty with sitting due to back pain and has gained weight as a result.
He used to enjoy playing golf but has had to stop due to his back pain.
Current medications: [NOT MENTIONED]
Objective:
Vital signs: [NOT MENTIONED]
Physical exam: The musculoskeletal back exam reveals no overlying erythema or ecchymosis.
There is midline tenderness at L4-L5 disk space with right-sided lumbar paravertebral tenderness.
The patient experiences pain with lumbar flexion and a positive straight leg test.
The neurological examination shows decreased sensation to light touch at the right lateral thigh at L4 dermatome.
Patellar and Achilles reflexes are symmetrical.
Test Results: The MRI results show disk herniation with nerve impingement.
Assessment / Problem List:
Assessment: The patient's symptoms are consistent with an L4-L5 herniated disk with radiculopathy.
Problem list: L4-L5 herniated disk with radiculopathy; chronic lumbar back pain; radiculopathy.
Plan:
I recommended a spinal injection with a corticosteroid to reduce inflammation causing the pain.
The corticosteroid will be injected into the lumbar spine under fluoroscopy.
This should help alleviate the pain.
We will monitor the patient's response to the injection and repeat as needed, not exceeding four times a year.
Follow-up: The patient will be scheduled for the first injection today.
